author: JB
text:
# Slice S3: Export (CSV + JSON)

You are implementing this slice in isolation in your own git worktree. Build all described behavior, write tests, and commit when done. Never block or wait for input — decide and continue.

## Backlog items

### F-17 — CSV export of the item register

**Rationale:** There is no way to get items out of the system for ordering or audit. listItems already funnels through serialize.ts; an export endpoint plus a download button is a natural read-path extension a real lab needs.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- The export endpoint returns a CSV body whose Content-Type is a CSV media type (e.g. text/csv)
- The CSV has a header row plus exactly 40 data rows for the seeded dataset, ordered by item name ascending
- The CSV includes at least item name, category, quantity and unit for each item
- An 'Export' control on the Items page triggers the download

**Hint files (touch-set):** `src/server/routes/items.ts`, `src/server/queries/items.ts`, `src/shared/serialize.ts`, `src/client/api.ts`, `src/client/containers/ItemsPage.tsx`

---
Work only within your assigned slice. Do not modify files outside your slice's scope.

author: JB
text: One scope note: the slice title says "CSV + JSON" but the only backlog item (F-17) specifies CSV. I implemented CSV fully and added JSON as a small `?format=json` extension to honor the title — it's behind the same endpoint and tested, not a separate surface.
